channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: NASA crashed a spacecraft into a space rock, accelerating two asteroids’ orbit around the sun - CNN
item.description:
1. NASA crashed a spacecraft into a space rock, accelerating two asteroids’ orbit around the sun  CNN
  2. In a first, NASA changed an asteroid’s orbit around the sun—by accident  National Geographic
  3. NASA successfully kicks asteroid off course in Earth defence test  France 24
  4. NASA’s DART Mission Changed Orbit of Asteroid Didymos Around Sun  NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) (.gov)
  5. NASA warns that over 15,000 'city-killing' asteroids are orbiting Earth undetected  Earth.com

item.title: Stormy space weather may be garbling messages from aliens, new research suggests - The Guardian
item.description:
1. Stormy space weather may be garbling messages from aliens, new research suggests  The Guardian
  2. Why SETI Might Have Been Missing Alien Signals  SETI
  3. SETI Thinks It Might Have Missed a Few Alien Calls. Here’s Why  Gizmodo
  4. Where are all the aliens? Maybe space weather is scrambling their transmissions  Space
  5. How ‘space weather’ may cause alien signals to get lost in space  The Mountaineer

item.title: How Nasa contractors are pressing on to bring humans to the moon with Artemis - The Guardian
item.description:
1. How Nasa contractors are pressing on to bring humans to the moon with Artemis  The Guardian
  2. NASA wants to accelerate its Artemis missions to the moon. It will need to drop some big hardware to do it.  Space
  3. NASA has shuffled its Artemis rockets. But what of the lunar landers?  Ars Technica
  4. Isaacman makes his mark by revamping the Artemis return to the moon  The Hill
  5. Is anyone else bored of Artemis already? Why I'm not over the Moon about NASA's new lunar missions  BBC Sky at Night Magazine

item.title: Space launches are changing the chemistry of Earth's atmosphere, studies warn. Here's what can be done - Phys.org
item.description:

  4. Don’t let mega-constellation-building billionaires steal your night sky  Scientific American
  5. Jeff Bezos' Amazon Urges FCC To Prevent Elon Musk's SpaceX Proposal To Launch A Million Satellites  Yahoo Finance

item.title: A Night Light in the Sky? Reflect Orbital Wants to Launch a Big Space Mirror. - The New York Times
item.description:
1. A Night Light in the Sky? Reflect Orbital Wants to Launch a Big Space Mirror.  The New York Times
  2. Space Companies Did Not Pollute Our Night Sky for Billions of Years and Should Not Do So in the…  Avi Loeb – Medium
  3. How satellites could be disrupting your sleep cycle  The Independent
  4. A project promising to illuminate the night with 50 mirrors in orbit is progressing in the US and opening a debate about light pollution, sleep, and space debris.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  5. Startup Wants Giant Orbital Mirrors to Turn Night Into Day – Scientists Are Horrified  Gadget Review

item.title: Strange signals are coming from the centre of our Galaxy. Scientists say they've worked out what they are - BBC Sky at Night Magazine
item.description:
1. Strange signals are coming from the centre of our Galaxy. Scientists say they've worked out what they are  BBC Sky at Night Magazine
  2. Three mysterious signals detected in the heart of the Milky Way have intrigued scientists for years, until a new study reveals that the explanation may lie in the enigmatic dark matter.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  3. Space breakthrough: Scientists uncover origin of three mysterious signals coming from Milky Way  GB News
  4. Scientists uncover the origin of three mysterious signals coming from the heart of the Milky Way - and say dark matter is responsible  Daily Mail
  5. Scientists pin source of three mysterious signals coming from centre of Milky Way to...  WION
